# UC-22.8.24 · SOC 2 CC8 — Infrastructure-as-Code Drift vs Approved Terraform Modules

## Description

CC8 governs change to infrastructure. Comparing live cloud resource tags and configurations to approved module signatures detects shadow infrastructure that bypassed pipeline controls—supporting change integrity for cloud environments.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cloud and sourcetypes aws:config_snapshot. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Adjust `spath` to your Config JSON shape; (2) maintain golden baseline per account; (3) integrate with Terraform Cloud audit API for module version; (4) route to cloud governance; (5) document exceptions in change register only. Indexes required: `index=cloud`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:config_snapshot`; Lookups: `approved_modules`, `approved_modules.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), AWS Config events via EventBridge HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cloud` `sourcetype="aws:config_snapshot"` (resourceId, resourceType, configuration, tags); `approved_modules.csv` (resourceType, required_tag_module, golden_hash).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Adjust `spath` to your Config JSON shape; (2) maintain golden baseline per account; (3) integrate with Terraform Cloud audit API for module version; (4) route to cloud governance; (5) document exceptions in change register only.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud sourcetype="aws:config_snapshot" resourceType="AWS::EC2::Instance" earliest=-1d
| spath path=configuration path=output
| eval module_tag=mvindex('tags{}.value', mvfind('tags{}.key', "TerraformModule"))
| lookup approved_modules.csv resourceType OUTPUT required_tag_module
| where isnull(module_tag) OR module_tag!=required_tag_module
| stats count by module_tag, resourceType, awsAccountId
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC8 — Infrastructure-as-Code Drift vs Approved Terraform Modules** — CC8 governs change to infrastructure. Comparing live cloud resource tags and configurations to approved module signatures detects shadow infrastructure that bypassed pipeline controls—supporting change integrity for cloud environments.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cloud` `sourcetype="aws:config_snapshot"` (resourceId, resourceType, configuration, tags); `approved_modules.csv` (resourceType, required_tag_module, golden_hash). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), AWS Config events via EventBridge HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud; **sourcetype**: aws:config_snapshot, AWS::EC2::Instance. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud, sourcetype="aws:config_snapshot", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Extracts structured paths (JSON/XML) with `spath`.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **module_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(module_tag) OR module_tag!=required_tag_module` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by module_tag, resourceType, awsAccountId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**SOC 2 CC8 — Infrastructure-as-Code Drift vs Approved Terraform Modules** — CC8 governs change to infrastructure. Comparing live cloud resource tags and configurations to approved module signatures detects shadow infrastructure that bypassed pipeline controls—supporting change integrity for cloud environments.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cloud` `sourcetype="aws:config_snapshot"` (resourceId, resourceType, configuration, tags); `approved_modules.csv` (resourceType, required_tag_module, golden_hash). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), AWS Config events via EventBridge HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (drift), Bar by account, Single value (instances noncompliant).

## SPL

```spl
index=cloud sourcetype="aws:config_snapshot" resourceType="AWS::EC2::Instance" earliest=-1d
| spath path=configuration path=output
| eval module_tag=mvindex('tags{}.value', mvfind('tags{}.key', "TerraformModule"))
| lookup approved_modules.csv resourceType OUTPUT required_tag_module
| where isnull(module_tag) OR module_tag!=required_tag_module
| stats count by module_tag, resourceType, awsAccountId
| sort - count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (drift), Bar by account, Single value (instances noncompliant). Key fields: `awsAccountId`, `module_tag`, `resourceType`.
